reviews.review_description:
As a long-time user of the Sketchar mobile app, I was genuinely excited about the Meta Quest VR version. The idea of using VR to replace a physical projector is compelling, and it’s exactly why I didn’t hesitate to spend $50 on a yearly Pro membership.

Unfortunately, in its current state, the VR app doesn’t yet deliver on that promise.

Several features demonstrated or implied in the tutorial aren’t functioning as expected. After selecting an image, the menu often becomes inaccessible, requiring a full restart just to continue. Essential tools for serious use—such as grid overlays, Z-axis positioning, and fine-grained adjustment controls—are missing. The experience is also unstable: images glitch, reset, or shift unexpectedly, and once that happens, it’s extremely difficult to realign them accurately.

That said, I want to be clear: I’m rooting for this to work. The concept is strong, and the ability to sync with the mobile app (which I’ve already invested in) shows real potential. I’m now close to $200 in across subscriptions and hardware because I believe in what Sketchar could be.

As a loyal user, my disappointment comes from high expectations, not dismissal. With stability improvements and the addition of basic alignment and control features, this could become a genuinely powerful tool for artists. I hope the team continues developing it, because I would love nothing more than to see this platform succeed.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the thoughtful review—and for being a long-time Sketchar user. We really appreciate the detail.

We’ve prepared a new VR version that addresses key issues you mentioned, and it’s scheduled to roll out in the coming weeks. We’d love for you to try it once it’s live.

reviews.review_title: Crashes if you try to resize
reviews.review_description:
Resizing or moving around the image you w
ant to trace  is an absolute nightmare. Sometimes if you try to resize then 2 other screens appear. Most of the important functions can only be performed if you pay the premium. Extremely counter-intuitive app.

developer_response.body: Hello. Please update the new version. It should be fixed. If you have any other suggestions, please drop us a line at hello@sketchar.io

reviews.review_description: Hello, I just bought the pro version and I can't save my image placement. When my headset turns off or the application closes, I have to start all over again and try to reposition the image where I began drawing.

developer_response.body: Your image stays exactly where you placed it — even when you take off the headset or it goes to sleep. The only rule: don't turn off the Quest with the power button, as that resets all app settings.

reviews.review_description: So thought this would be fun but for some reason it won't let me install on meta quest 2. Any help with this would be great

reviews.review_title: Love the concept but needs fixing
reviews.review_description: Got the meta solely for this app. I’ve tried it a few times and for the most part it works, but even if I lock the image and remove the glasses even for a second if completely erases the image from the vr and I have to start over, somehow placing the image exactly where it was before which is really hard to do. I haven’t signed up for the paid version yet because I wanted to try it first with the stock images first to get a feel for it.

I did pay and was charged for a pro subscription but it never activated.
Other than that the app seems to be good but unusable because of subscription inactivity

developer_response.body: The bug has been fixed — please update the app and give it another try! We hope everything works smoothly now, and we'd love it if you'd consider updating your review. 😊

reviews.review_description:
Its a png viewer. That is literally it.
Not a drawing app, not a useful tool. You can literally already do everything this app does using your browser and file manager alone.
Bad marketing, poor explanation, underwhelming product.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback!

While we appreciate that it may look like “just a PNG viewer” at first glance, Sketchar is actually the pioneer of AR Drawing — and we’re on a mission to let people create art in the real world using immersive technology. Meta Quest is just the beginning of expanding that vision beyond mobile.

We’re always evolving (we did invent meaningful AR drawing in 2012, after all), and your input helps us grow — even when it stings a little.

Stay curious,
The Sketchar Team

reviews.review_description: good
its pretty cool
W gam get it

This app is incredible the only thing I’d say is that we have more control on how small we can put the stencils there appears to be a lock at a certain small size and should be able to go smaller for smaller note pads

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much — we’re glad you’re enjoying the app!

And great news: we’ve heard your feedback, and the limitation on templates size has been removed in the latest update. Now you can scale templates down freely — from small notepads to the huge walls.

Keep creating,
The Sketchar Team 🎨
